qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated GraphQL playground endpoint exposes Parse Server master key in client-side JavaScript
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SQL injection via Increment operations on nested object fields in the PostgreSQL storage adapter (CWE-89). This finding describes information disclosure of the master key via the GraphQL playground endpoint (src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js). The root cause, vulnerable code path, and vulnerability class are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-q3vj-96h2-gwvg) describes a SQL injection vulnerability in the PostgreSQL storage adapter of Parse Server, specifically in the handling of Increment operations on nested object fields using dot notation where the amount value is interpolated directly into SQL without parameterization. The sole qualifying finding ('graphql-playground-master-key-exposure') describes master key disclosure via the GraphQL playground endpoint (src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js), which is an information disclosure vulnerability in a completely different component with a different root cause and vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory identifies SQL injection (CWE-89) in PostgreSQL storage adapter via Increment operation on nested object fields with dot notation.
Finding 'graphql-playground-master-key-exposure' targets GraphQL playground master key disclosure (src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js) - unrelated code path and vulnerability class.
